summary: Iteration 5 backend testing complete. Verified governance gating, lightweight dashboard stats, and the new approve-and-forward chain endpoint, plus terminal /approve chain-close behaviour. 23/23 pytest cases pass.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 1294 lines — exceeds the 700-line guideline. Consider splitting routers (auth, submissions, analytics, files, notifications) into separate modules for maintainability.
/approve does not enforce a state-machine guard (e.g., can approve from any non-terminal status). Known/accepted minor issue per the iteration-5 brief.

MAX_CHAIN_DEPTH guard exists in /approve-and-forward (limit 10) but is hard to exercise end-to-end given chain only forwards between non-CEO approver roles (reviewer, marketing_lead, vp); covered by code inspection rather than runtime test.

EscalateIn pydantic model accepts an optional assigned_user_id, so /approve-and-forward returns 400 ('assigned_user_id required to forward') instead of 422 when omitted — test tolerates both. Consider making the field required at the schema level for /approve-and-forward via a dedicated request model.
/approve does not also reset stage_durations or include forwarded_to_* nulls on its chain entry — clients should treat missing forwarded_to_id as 'terminal'. Tests handle this.
Submitter does not receive a separate notification kind to differentiate 'approved-with-forward' from 'fully-approved' (both use kind='approved'). Consider distinct kinds for clearer UX timelines.
test_credentials: submitter@govern.app / reviewer@govern.app / lead@govern.app / vp@govern.app / ceo@govern.app — all Pass1234! (read from /app/memory/test_credentials.md)
seed_data_creation: Created TEST_IT5_* submissions and a few alt users via /auth/register during chain tests. No cleanup performed — naming prefix allows identification.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 5 added 3 things, all verified: (1) /api/analytics/overview now 403s for submitter/reviewer/marketing_lead and 200 for vp/ceo; (2) new GET /api/dashboard/stats returns {total, by_status, avg_approval_hours, completed_count} for all authenticated roles; (3) POST /api/submissions/{id}/approve-and-forward records an open chain entry (closed:false) and reassigns; /approve appends a final closed:true entry. End-to-end 3-step chain (reviewer -> lead -> vp -> approve) results in approval_chain length 3 with only the last entry closed=true. Test suite at /app/backend/tests/test_iteration5_features.py is reusable as regression.
